qas.q: What is the definition of PKI?
qas.a: A set of hardware, software, people, policies and procedures needed to create, manage, distribute, use, store and revoke digital certificate

qas.q: Explain two factor or dual factor authentication?
qas.a: Something you have & something you know or CAC + PIN

qas.q: What directive will you follow when reporting incidents?
What directive will you follow for vulnerability management?
Understand the differences between signature and anomaly.
qas.a:
Signature Based - Signature-Based IDS use a rule set to identify intrusions by watching for patterns of events specific to known and documented attacks. It is typically connected to a large database which houses attack signatures.

Anomaly-Based IDS examines ongoing traffic, activity, transactions and behavior in order to identify intrusions by detecting anomalies. Requires an established baseline of normal behavior.
